message.content: Dimension: SUPPLY-CHAIN / CI. Probe /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github + scripts + lockfiles for gate completeness/reproducibility, least-privilege, SBOM/license/dependabot, pinning. If nothing, state what you checked. Return JSON findings: id (P7-<dim>-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix. ONLY genuine reproducible defects — do not manufacture nits.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll probe the supply-chain/CI surface of the hc-assay project.

input.command: find /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github -type f 2>/dev/null; echo "---SCRIPTS---"; ls -la /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts 2>/dev/null; echo "---LOCKFILES---"; ls -la /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/*.lock /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/*.txt /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/poetry.lock /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/uv.lock /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/Pipfile* /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/package-lock.json 2>/dev/null; echo "---ROOT---"; ls -la /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay 2>/dev/null

1	name: ci
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	# Least privilege (#H-008): the default GITHUB_TOKEN can be repo-write; this workflow only reads
9	# the code and uploads artifacts, so pin the token to read-only at the top level. Any job needing
10	# more must opt in explicitly with its own `permissions:`.
11	permissions:
12	  contents: read
13	
14	jobs:
15	  # Dependency-free core: the engine must lint, type-check, and test with NO extras installed
16	  # (optional backends are import-guarded; integration tests skip). This guards ADR-0006.
17	  core:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    strategy:
20	      matrix:
21	        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13", "3.14"]
22	    steps:
23	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
24	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
25	        with:
26	          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
27	      - name: Install (no extras, hash-pinned)
28	        run: |
29	          # Install the dev toolchain from the hashed, audited core lockfile so tests run against
30	          # EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes — not a live PyPI
31	          # resolution where a malicious patch release could execute under test (#F-004). The
32	          # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
33	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
34	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
35	          pip install --no-deps -e .
36	      - name: Lint (ruff)
37	        run: ruff check src tests
38	      - name: Format check (ruff)
39	        run: ruff format --check src tests
40	      - name: Type-check (mypy)
41	        run: mypy
42	      - name: Test (pytest)
43	        run: pytest -q
44	
45	  # Extras lane: install the real backends and run the integration suite so the backend code
46	  # paths actually execute, and mypy type-checks against the REAL (not ignored) library types.
47	  # Service-dependent tests (ollama/qdrant/postgres) skip when the service is absent in CI; the
48	  # extra-only tests (langgraph graph, mlflow lifecycle, OTel, numpy primitives, methodology
49	  # e2e) run for real.
50	  integration:
51	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
52	    services:
53	      postgres:
54	        image: postgres:16
55	        env:
56	          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
57	          POSTGRES_DB: assay
58	        ports: ["5432:5432"]
59	        options: >-
60	          --health-cmd "pg_isready -U postgres"
61	          --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
62	      qdrant:
63	        image: qdrant/qdrant:v1.12.4
64	        ports: ["6333:6333"]
65	    env:
66	      # loopback host keeps the data-sovereignty guard satisfied; creds are redacted on error
67	      ASSAY_TEST_POSTGRES_URL: postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/assay
68	    steps:
69	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
70	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
71	        with:
72	          python-version: "3.12"
73	      - name: Install (all extras, hash-pinned)
74	        run: |
75	          # Install from the hashed all-extras lockfile so the integration suite executes against
76	          # exactly the audited, pinned versions — closing the window where a compromised patch
77	          # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
78	          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
79	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
80	          pip install --no-deps -e .
81	      - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
82	        run: mypy
83	      - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
84	        # #H-009: the live Postgres/Qdrant tests SKIP when the service is unreachable, so a
85	        # misconfigured/never-started container would let the integration job report GREEN without
86	        # actually exercising the backends. Fail loudly here if either service isn't up, so the
87	        # gate cannot pass vacuously.
88	        run: |
89	          set -euo pipefail
90	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
91	            pg_isready -h localhost -p 5432 -U postgres && break || sleep 2
92	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Postgres service never became ready"; exit 1; }
93	          done
94	          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
95	            curl -fsS http://localhost:6333/readyz && break || sleep 2
96	            [ "$i" = "30" ] && { echo "::error::Qdrant service never became ready"; exit 1; }
97	          done
98	          echo "postgres + qdrant reachable"
99	      # Real backends now run: langgraph graph + interrupt/resume, mlflow lifecycle, OTel,
100	      # numpy primitives, the T3 drain/classification (synthetic SDK, no metered call), AND —
101	      # via the service containers — the live Postgres checkpointer + Qdrant vector store.
102	      # Ollama and the metered frontier tier are not in CI and skip honestly.
103	      - name: Integration + unit tests with coverage
104	        run: pytest -q --cov=assay_engine --cov-report=term-missing
105	
106	  # Supply-chain: fail on known-vulnerable dependencies (a security/privacy engine must not ship
107	  # with advisories). The scan runs against the committed, fully-pinned, hashed lockfile
108	  # (requirements.lock) so the audit is reproducible across runs and matches what installs
109	  # (#108, #130, #131, #132, #145, #146). An allowlist file (.pip-audit-ignore, one advisory id
110	  # per line, '#' comments and blank lines allowed) covers accepted/unfixable ones.
111	  audit:
112	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
113	    steps:
114	      - uses: actions/checkout@34e114876b0b11c390a56381ad16ebd13914f8d5 # v4
115	      - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5
116	        with:
117	          python-version: "3.12"
118	      - name: Install pip-audit
119	        # Pin the audit tooling to exact versions (#G-022): an unpinned pip-audit can change
120	        # advisory results / exit behavior between releases, making the supply-chain gate
121	        # non-reproducible (a green commit could fail tomorrow on a tooling bump) — the analogue
122	        # of the uv pin (#F-031).
123	        run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip && python -m pip install pip-audit==2.10.1
124	      - name: Lockfiles are in sync with pyproject (reproducibility guard)
125	        run: |
126	          # Pin uv to the exact version the lockfiles were generated with (#F-031): an unpinned
127	          # `uv` upgrade can change output format/ordering/annotations and produce spurious diffs
128	          # that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current.
129	          python -m pip install uv==0.11.21
130	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements.regen.lock
131	          # ignore the auto-generated header (timestamp/command line) when diffing
132	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements.regen.lock) \
133	            || { echo "::error::requirements.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock"; exit 1; }
134	          # the core lockfile (dev extra only) backs the hash-pinned core lane (#F-004) and must
135	          # stay in sync too.
136	          uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
137	          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
138	            || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
139	      - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
140	        run: |
141	          set -euo pipefail
142	          # --strict: any dependency that cannot be collected/audited FAILS the gate (no silent
143	          # skips). --require-hashes: every pin must carry a hash. Build --ignore-vuln args as an
144	          # array so allowlist entries can't word-split or glob-expand, and read the file
145	          # newline-agnostically so the last line is honored even without a trailing newline.
146	          IGNORES=()
147	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
148	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
149	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
150	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
151	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
152	          fi
153	          pip-audit --strict --require-hashes --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock "${IGNORES[@]}"
154	      - name: SBOM (CycloneDX) from the lockfile
155	        run: |
156	          set -euo pipefail
157	          # Emit a machine-readable software bill of materials for the pinned dependency set so
158	          # downstream license/transitive monitoring has a stable artifact to track (#147). No
159	          # `|| true` mask (#F-012): a pip-audit failure must fail the step, and the SBOM must be
160	          # non-empty, so a broken/absent SBOM cannot pass CI green.
161	          # Apply the SAME allowlist as the gate (#G-015): pip-audit exits non-zero on ANY finding
162	          # regardless of output format, so without the allowlist an ACCEPTED advisory would fail
163	          # SBOM generation here even though the gate step passed — coupling SBOM emission to vuln
164	          # status and making the allowlist ineffective. The gate step already ran first, so a
165	          # NON-allowlisted advisory has failed the job before we reach here.
166	          IGNORES=()
167	          if [ -f .pip-audit-ignore ]; then
168	            while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
169	              case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
170	              IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
171	            done < .pip-audit-ignore
172	          fi
173	          pip-audit --progress-spinner off -r requirements.lock --format cyclonedx-json -o sbom.cyclonedx.json "${IGNORES[@]}"
174	          test -s sbom.cyclonedx.json
175	          echo "SBOM emitted ($(wc -c < sbom.cyclonedx.json) bytes)"
176	      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
177	        if: always()
178	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
179	        with:
180	          name: sbom-cyclonedx
181	          path: sbom.cyclonedx.json
182	          if-no-files-found: error
183	      - name: License gate (deny strong-copyleft in the pinned dependency set)
184	        run: |
185	          set -euo pipefail
186	          # Fail if any dependency carries a strong-copyleft license incompatible with the
187	          # Apache-2.0 distribution (#147). Scan the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a
188	          # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
189	          # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
190	          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
191	          pip install --no-deps -e .
192	          python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
193	          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
194	          python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
195	      - name: Upload license report
196	        if: always()
197	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4
198	        with:
199	          name: license-report
200	          path: licenses.json
201	          if-no-files-found: warn
202	
203	  # Single required status check (#F-014): branch protection should require ONLY `all-checks`.
204	  # It fans in every other job, so adding a new job automatically gates merges without anyone
205	  # remembering to update the branch-protection rule — and `audit`/`integration` can never be
206	  # silently dropped from the merge gate by requiring just `core`.
207	  all-checks:
208	    if: always()
209	    needs: [core, integration, audit]
210	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
211	    steps:
212	      - name: Verify all required jobs succeeded
213	        run: |
214	          results="${{ needs.core.result }} ${{ needs.integration.result }} ${{ needs.audit.result }}"
215	          echo "job results: $results"
216	          for r in $results; do
217	            if [ "$r" != "success" ]; then
218	              echo "::error::a required job did not succeed ($r)"
219	              exit 1
220	            fi
221	          done
222	          echo "all required jobs succeeded"
223

1	# Automated dependency + transitive-update monitoring (#147). Dependabot opens PRs for
2	# vulnerable/outdated dependencies on a weekly cadence so the supply-chain surface is tracked,
3	# not just scanned once. Pairs with the pip-audit gate (reproducible scan of requirements.lock)
4	# and the CycloneDX SBOM artifact in ci.yml.
5	#
6	# NOTE (#G-016): Dependabot bumps the ranges in pyproject.toml but does NOT regenerate the
7	# hash-pinned uv lockfiles (requirements.lock / requirements-core.lock), so a Dependabot PR will
8	# FAIL the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until a maintainer regenerates them. On a
9	# Dependabot PR, run `scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh` and commit the result to make it mergeable.
10	version: 2
11	updates:
12	  # Python packages declared in pyproject.toml (and resolved into requirements.lock).
13	  - package-ecosystem: pip
14	    directory: "/"
15	    schedule:
16	      interval: weekly
17	    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
18	    groups:
19	      # batch low-risk updates so the queue stays reviewable
20	      minor-and-patch:
21	        update-types: ["minor", "patch"]
22	  # The GitHub Actions used by the CI workflows themselves.
23	  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
24	    directory: "/"
25	    schedule:
26	      interval: weekly
27

1	"""License gate (#147): fail if any installed dependency carries a strong-copyleft license.
2	
3	Reads a pip-licenses JSON report (path as argv[1], default ``licenses.json``) and exits non-zero
4	if any package's license matches a denied strong-copyleft family that would be incompatible with
5	the Apache-2.0 distribution. LGPL (weak copyleft, dynamic linking) is permitted. Advisory
6	monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import json
12	import sys
13	
14	# Strong-copyleft families denied in the distributed dependency set. Both the SPDX-style short
15	# tokens AND the full prose names must be listed (pass 3, #F-015): a package reporting
16	# "Server Side Public License" or "European Union Public Licence 1.2" contains neither the "SSPL"
17	# nor "EUPL" token, so the short tokens alone let those families bypass the gate.
18	_DENY = (
19	    "GPL",
20	    "AGPL",
21	    "SSPL",
22	    "EUPL",
23	    "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC",  # SSPL prose form
24	    "EUROPEAN UNION PUBLIC",  # EUPL prose form
25	    "AFFERO",  # AGPL prose form ("GNU Affero General Public License")
26	)
27	
28	
29	def main(path: str = "licenses.json") -> int:
30	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:  # context manager — no leaked fd (#F-050)
31	        packages = json.load(f)
32	    bad = []
33	    unknown = []
34	    for pkg in packages:
35	        raw = (pkg.get("License") or "").strip()
36	        lic = raw.upper()
37	        # A package whose license is empty / UNKNOWN / unspecified could be hiding a copyleft the
38	        # deny-substring scan can't see. Don't fail on it (pip-licenses reports UNKNOWN for many
39	        # legitimate packages with incomplete metadata), but SURFACE it as a CI warning so a
40	        # reviewer checks it instead of it passing silently (#H-007).
41	        if not raw or lic in ("UNKNOWN", "UNKNOWN LICENSE", "NONE"):
42	            unknown.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}")
43	            continue
44	        if "LGPL" in lic:  # weak copyleft — permitted for dynamically-linked libraries
45	            continue
46	        if any(tok in lic for tok in _DENY):
47	            bad.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}: {pkg.get('License')}")
48	    if unknown:
49	        print(
50	            "::warning::license gate: "
51	            + str(len(unknown))
52	            + " package(s) report no/UNKNOWN license — review for hidden copyleft: "
53	            + ", ".join(sorted(unknown))
54	        )
55	    if bad:
56	        print("::error::strong-copyleft licenses found:\n" + "\n".join(bad))
57	        return 1
58	    print(f"license gate OK ({len(packages)} packages checked, {len(unknown)} unknown)")
59	    return 0
60	
61	
62	if __name__ == "__main__":
63	    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "licenses.json"))
64

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Regenerate the hash-pinned lockfiles from pyproject.toml (#G-016).
3	#
4	# Dependabot bumps the version ranges in pyproject.toml but cannot regenerate the uv lockfiles,
5	# so a Dependabot PR will fail the CI "requirements.lock is stale" gate until the lockfiles are
6	# regenerated. Run this on a Dependabot branch (or any PR that changes pyproject dependencies),
7	# then commit the updated requirements*.lock files:
8	#
9	#     scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh && git commit -am "chore: regenerate lockfiles"
10	#
11	# uv is pinned to the same version CI uses (#F-031) so the output matches the sync check exactly.
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	UV_VERSION="0.11.21"
15	
16	if ! command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$(uv --version | awk '{print $2}')" != "$UV_VERSION" ]; then
17	  echo "installing uv==$UV_VERSION (CI-pinned) ..." >&2
18	  python -m pip install "uv==$UV_VERSION" >&2
19	fi
20	
21	uv pip compile pyproject.toml --all-extras --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements.lock
22	uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock
23	
24	echo "regenerated requirements.lock + requirements-core.lock — review and commit them."
25

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Provenance: snapshot the active Claude session transcript(s) into transcripts/ on every commit
# so each commit carries an auditable record of the agent interactions/effort behind it.
# Best-effort — never block a commit.
set -u
ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null)" || exit 0
PY="$ROOT/.venv/bin/python"
[ -x "$PY" ] || PY="$(command -v python3 || true)"
[ -n "$PY" ] || exit 0
"$PY" "$ROOT/scripts/capture_transcripts.py" 2>/dev/null || true
git add -- "$ROOT/transcripts" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0

Let me check a more concrete issue: the SBOM and license report uploads run `if: always()` but their generation steps are gated on prior success.

Let me look at whether `pip install --no-deps -e .` with a dynamic hatchling version build requires build deps not in the hashed lockfile — a genuine reproducibility gap.

content.text: I found a genuine reproducibility defect: `hatchling` is the build backend but is NOT in either hash-pinned lockfile. When CI runs `pip install --no-deps -e .`, pip builds the editable wheel using PEP 517 build isolation, which fetches `hatchling` (and its deps) live from PyPI un-pinned and un-hashed. This breaks the "EXACTLY the versions audited" reproducibility claim and opens the exact supply-chain window (#F-004) the workflow says it closes. Let me confirm.

Confirmed: `hatchling`, `editables`, and `trove-classifiers` (hatchling's runtime deps) are NOT in either lockfile. `pathspec`/`pluggy` happen to be present as transitive runtime deps but `hatchling` itself is absent. So `pip install --no-deps -e .` with default build isolation will fetch `hatchling` and `editables` live, un-pinned and un-hashed. This is a genuine reproducibility/supply-chain gap that contradicts the workflow's own stated guarantee.

content.text:
Actions are all SHA-pinned. The license gate `pip-licenses==5.5.5` is installed without `--require-hashes` in an already-hashed env — pip allows this only because the env wasn't created in `--require-hashes` constraint mode per-invocation; it's a minor pinning gap (version pinned, no hash) but reproducible enough. The substantive finding is the build-backend (hatchling) not being pinned/hashed/audited.

Let me also note a real least-privilege observation: the `audit` job uploads artifacts but the top-level `permissions: contents: read` is fine. No genuine privilege defect there. And `concurrency` absence is a minor efficiency/robustness nit, not a security defect — I'll exclude it.

Let me do one final confirmation of the license gate `--with-system` env-scan claim being a real divergence from the lockfile that the comment claims.

content.text: I have enough to report. Let me submit findings.

findings.title: Build backend (hatchling) fetched live and un-hashed during `pip install -e .`, defeating the reproducibility/hash-pin guarantee
findings.description: All three jobs install the package with `pip install --no-deps -e .` (lines 35, 80, 191). `--no-deps` suppresses runtime dependency resolution but does NOT disable PEP 517 build isolation. Because pyproject.toml declares `build-system.requires = ["hatchling"]` (pyproject.toml:1-3) and the version is dynamic (computed by hatchling at build time), pip creates an isolated build environment and fetches `hatchling` and its deps (`editables`, `trove-classifiers`, etc.) LIVE from PyPI, un-pinned and un-hashed, every CI run. Neither `requirements.lock` nor `requirements-core.lock` contains `hatchling`/`editables`/`trove-classifiers` (confirmed: 0 matches). Consequences: (1) The workflow's central claim — installing 'EXACTLY the versions pip-audit scanned and the SBOM describes' (lines 29-32, 76-77) — is false for the build toolchain; (2) pip-audit (`-r requirements.lock`) never sees hatchling, so a compromised hatchling/editables patch release executes arbitrary code during the editable build in CI yet is never scanned — the exact `#F-004` supply-chain window the comments claim is closed; (3) the SBOM omits the build-time dependency set; (4) a yanked/changed hatchling release makes a previously-green commit non-reproducible.
findings.poc: In CI on a clean runner, `pip install --no-deps -e .` triggers `Getting requirements to build editable ... Installing build dependencies: hatchling, editables, ...` resolved live from PyPI with no `--require-hashes` and no lock entry. Verify: `grep -ic '^hatchling==' requirements.lock requirements-core.lock` -> 0 for both, while the package still builds via hatchling.
findings.suggested_fix: Include the build backend in the hash-pinned set and disable live build isolation in CI. Either (a) add hatchling to the locks (e.g. `uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes` after adding hatchling to a build/dev group) and install with `pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .` so the already-hashed hatchling is used; or (b) build a wheel in a separately-pinned, hashed build env and `pip install` that wheel. Also extend the SBOM/pip-audit coverage to the build toolchain.

findings.title: License gate scans entire site-packages (`--with-system`), not the hash-pinned set the comment claims
findings.description: The license-gate step comment (lines 184-189) asserts it scans 'the SAME hash-pinned set pip-audit audited, not a live resolution.' In practice the command is `pip-licenses --format=json --with-system` (line 193), which enumerates EVERY distribution in site-packages. By that point the env also contains `pip-licenses==5.5.5` and its transitive deps (prettytable, wcwidth, etc.) installed at line 192, plus pip/setuptools/wheel via `--with-system`, plus the live-fetched build backend. So the license-checked set is a superset of `requirements.lock`, contradicting the comment and coupling the production-distribution license verdict to dev/tooling packages. A copyleft license appearing in a pip-licenses transitive dep (or any tool dep) would spuriously fail the gate, or conversely could be (mis)read as part of the distributed set. Additionally, `pip-licenses==5.5.5` is version-pinned but installed without `--require-hashes` (line 192), unlike every other install in this job — a minor pinning inconsistency.
findings.poc: `pip-licenses --with-system` output length != number of packages in requirements.lock; it includes pip-licenses, prettytable, wcwidth, pip, setuptools, wheel and the build backend, none of which are in requirements.lock.
findings.suggested_fix: Scan only the pinned distribution set: run `pip-licenses` in/against the locked environment without `--with-system`, or drive it from `requirements.lock` package names (e.g. `pip-licenses --packages $(...)`), and install pip-licenses in a separate step/venv so tooling deps never enter the scanned environment. Add `--require-hashes` (with a hash) for the pip-licenses install to match the rest of the job.

content.text:
I completed the supply-chain/CI probe of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

What I checked:
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (full CI: core matrix, integration, audit, all-checks fan-in)
- `.github/dependabot.yml` (pip + github-actions, weekly)
- `scripts/license_gate.py`, `scripts/regenerate_lockfiles.sh`, `scripts/capture_transcripts.py`
- `.githooks/pre-commit`
- `pyproject.toml` (build-system, deps, extras)
- `requirements.lock`, `requirements-core.lock`, `uv.lock`

What was already solid (no findings): all third-party actions are SHA-pinned with version comments; top-level `permissions: contents: read` least-privilege; `--require-hashes` enforced on the main installs; pip-audit `--strict --require-hashes` with array-built allowlist; reproducibility gate that re-runs `uv pip compile` with pinned `uv==0.11.21` and diffs; CycloneDX SBOM emitted with no `|| true` mask and non-empty assertion; license gate covering both SPDX tokens and prose forms; vacuity guard that fails if Postgres/Qdrant containers never come up; `all-checks` umbrella so branch protection needs only one required check.

Genuine defects found (2):

1. P7-SUPPLY-CHAIN-1 (medium) — `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.github/workflows/ci.yml:35,80,191`. `pip install --no-deps -e .` does not disable PEP 517 build isolation, so `hatchling` (+ `editables`, `trove-classifiers`) is fetched LIVE and un-hashed from PyPI every run. Neither lockfile contains it (0 matches), pip-audit never scans it, and the SBOM omits it — defeating the workflow's own "exactly the audited versions" / #F-004 claim and leaving a build-time code-execution window. Fix: pin the build backend into the hashed locks and use `--no-build-isolation`.

2. P7-SUPPLY-CHAIN-2 (low) — `ci.yml:192-193`. License gate uses `pip-licenses --with-system`, scanning all of site-packages (including pip-licenses' own deps, system tools, and the live build backend) rather than the hash-pinned lockfile set the comment claims; can spuriously pass/fail on tooling licenses. Also `pip-licenses==5.5.5` is installed without `--require-hashes`, unlike the rest of the job.

I did not manufacture nits: I deliberately excluded the missing `concurrency`/`cancel-in-progress` block and default `persist-credentials: true` on checkout, since neither is a reproducible security/correctness defect in this read-only, non-deploying workflow.
